description: Synthesis cadence broken -- 14 signals queued, 0 synthesis docs in 14d (threshold 10/72h crossed). Root-cause why /synthesize-signals is not firing autonomously and ship a fix (cron, dispatcher routine, or producer-on-threshold trigger).
goal_context: TELOS contradiction surfaced 2026-04-08: STATUS.md was claiming Learning loop FLOWING/RECOVERING but evidence shows producers EMIT, synthesis NEVER FIRES. Pipeline emits telemetry, not learning. Honest STATUS.md updated same session; this task fixes the underlying cadence.

notes:
Filed from morning brief Step 4 TELOS scan. Eric chose option (c) -- update STATUS now + file root-cause task. Linked: morning brief Top 3 #3.

2026-04-08 RESOLVED: Root cause = (1) heartbeat signal_velocity remediation only fires on level CHANGE; cooldown locked at 2026-04-02T00:00:03Z so it has been dormant 6 days; (2) hook_session_start.py correctly detects synthesis-due (10 signals/72h tier) but only PRINTS, no producer wires it to a backlog task; (3) the existing weekly-synthesis routine was using the wrong skill (learning-capture instead of synthesize-signals) and a 7-day interval that doesn't match the 72h threshold. FIX SHIPPED: weekly-synthesis routine updated -- skill=synthesize-signals, interval_days=3 (matches 72h tier), new executable verifier tools/scripts/verify_synthesis_routine.py supports Idle Is Success. routine_state reset to 2026-04-04 so it fires on next dispatcher run. inject_routines() invocation confirmed task-1775676329399322 in backlog as pending. v2 (true threshold-aware firing via routine condition block) filed as separate task.

description: Run /synthesize-signals to distill accumulated learning signals into a synthesis doc
goal_context: Learning compounds -- threshold model is 10 signals / 72h. This routine fires every 3 days so the worst case is one missed cycle. If no signals are queued, /synthesize-signals exits Idle Is Success.

Tier 1 + expected_outputs declares the write surface for scope_creep.

description: Add `condition` block to routines.json schema for state-aware firing (v2 of synthesis cadence fix)
goal_context: Today's fix uses calendar-only firing (interval_days=3) which still wastes one worker call when no signals are queued, and is too coarse for bursty signal arrival. v2: routines engine evaluates an optional `condition` Python callable BEFORE injecting -- only inject if condition returns True. Lets weekly-synthesis fire on actual signal-due state instead of calendar.
isc: routines.json _schema documents the new `condition` field with example | Verify: Review
tools/scripts/jarvis_dispatcher.py inject_routines() evaluates condition before injecting | Verify: Review
weekly-synthesis migrated to use condition instead of interval_days | Verify: Review
notes: v2 follow-up to task-775675072729339. Calendar firing is the v1 floor; condition-based firing is the v2 ceiling.

description: backlog_append id-generator collides under Windows clock resolution -- fix and repair history. Root cause: tools/scripts/lib/backlog.py line 265 uses int(time.time() * 1_000_000), but on Windows time.time() resolution is ~15ms (system tick), so successive calls within 15ms hit the same microsecond bucket. Discovered 2026-04-08 when /architecture-review filed 4 replacement tasks and 3 of 4 got the same id (task-1775687205721793). Historical audit shows 1 prior collision: task-1775467802350228 has 5 distinct done records sharing it (created 2026-04-06 from a batch routine injection). Fix: switch generator to time.time_ns() // 1000 and add a process-local monotonic counter that bumps when the same microsecond is reused. Also: write a one-shot repair script that finds remaining historical collisions and renames duplicates with deterministic suffixes (preserving the lexically-first occurrence).
goal_context: Phase 5C data integrity -- the backlog is the substrate every autonomous loop reads. Two concurrent producers within 15ms of each other lose row identity. Done records are the immediate observable damage, but any active-task selection on the colliding id would also corrupt.
isc: tools/scripts/lib/backlog.py id generator uses time.time_ns() (or equivalent monotonic ns source) and adds a process-local disambiguation counter for same-bucket calls | Verify: grep -n "time_ns\|disambig\|_LAST_ID" tools/scripts/lib/backlog.py

A self-test or pytest case demonstrates 100 successive backlog_append calls produce 100 distinct ids on Windows | Verify: pytest tests/defensive/test_backlog_id_uniqueness.py OR python tools/scripts/lib/backlog.py --self-test

A repair script tools/scripts/repair_backlog_dupe_ids.py finds all colliding ids in the backlog, renames duplicates with deterministic suffixes, and writes the result atomically | Verify: ls tools/scripts/repair_backlog_dupe_ids.py

After running the repair script, all ids in orchestration/task_backlog.jsonl are unique | Verify: python -c "import json; ids=[json.loads(l)["id"] for l in open("orchestration/task_backlog.jsonl",encoding="utf-8") if l.strip()]; assert len(ids)==len(set(ids)), "dupes remain""
NOT: any code path in backlog.py uses int(time.time() * 1_000_000) for id generation | Verify: grep -n "time.time() \* 1_000_000\|int(time.time" tools/scripts/lib/backlog.py | wc -l == 0
notes: Side finding from /architecture-review on Managed Agents absorb patterns 2026-04-08. Discovered when filing 4 replacement tasks via backlog_append: 3 of 4 collided on the same auto-generated id. Inline data-fix already shipped (renamed 2 of the 3 collisions in this session). Historical audit: task-1775467802350228 has 5 done records with the same id from 2026-04-06 routine batch injection. PRIORITY 2: data integrity bug on the substrate every autonomous producer writes to. Repair script in this task fixes the historical 5-way collision too.
